courses.name: AP Physics 1
courses.subject: physics
units: Energy and momentum of rotating systems

courses.name: AP Physics 2
courses.subject: physics
units: Magnetic forces, magnetic fields, and Faraday's law

courses.name: High school physics
courses.subject: physics
units: Forces and Newton's laws of motion

courses.name: Physics library
courses.subject: physics
units: Forces and Newton's laws of motion
Magnetic forces, magnetic fields, and Faraday's law

concepts.title: Angular kinematics
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Rotational analogs of linear kinematics: angular position, angular velocity, and angular acceleration.

concepts.title: Angular momentum and its conservation
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Energy and momentum of rotating systems
concepts.description: The rotational analog of linear momentum, conserved when no external torque acts, explaining spinning skaters and gyroscopes.

concepts.title: Buoyancy and Archimedes' principle
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The upward force on a submerged or floating object equal to the weight of the fluid it displaces.

concepts.title: Center of mass
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The mass-weighted average position of a system, which moves as though all mass and external force act there.

concepts.title: Continuity and Bernoulli's principle
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Conservation of mass (continuity) and energy (Bernoulli) in flowing fluids, relating speed, pressure, and elevation.

concepts.title: Fluid density and pressure
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: How pressure in a static fluid increases with depth and acts equally in all directions.

concepts.title: Gravitational fields and orbital motion
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Gravitational field strength and the use of gravity as a centripetal force to sustain circular and elliptical orbits.

concepts.title: Moment of inertia
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Energy and momentum of rotating systems
concepts.description: An object's resistance to angular acceleration, depending on its mass distribution relative to the axis of rotation.

concepts.title: Energy in oscillators (pendulum and spring)
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: How energy continuously transforms between kinetic and potential forms in simple harmonic oscillators, and what sets their period.

concepts.title: Newton's second law for rotation
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Energy and momentum of rotating systems
concepts.description: Net torque equals moment of inertia times angular acceleration, the rotational form of Newton's second law.

concepts.title: Rotational kinetic energy and rolling
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Energy and momentum of rotating systems
concepts.description: Energy of a rotating body and the combined translational-plus-rotational energy of objects rolling without slipping.

concepts.title: Static equilibrium and torque balance
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Conditions for an extended body to be in equilibrium: net force and net torque both zero, as with balanced beams and ladders.

concepts.title: Torque
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The rotational equivalent of force, equal to the force times the lever arm, that causes angular acceleration.

concepts.title: Atomic energy levels and spectra
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Electrons occupy discrete energy levels; transitions between them emit or absorb photons of specific wavelengths, producing line spectra.

concepts.title: Capacitors and dielectrics
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Devices that store charge and energy in an electric field between conducting plates, and how dielectrics increase capacitance.

concepts.title: Interference and diffraction of light
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Wave behavior of light producing interference fringes (double slit) and diffraction patterns, evidence of light's wave nature.

concepts.title: Electric potential and voltage
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The electric potential energy per unit charge, its relationship to the electric field, and equipotential surfaces.

concepts.title: Faraday's law and induction
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: A changing magnetic flux through a loop induces an EMF and current, with direction set by Lenz's law.

concepts.title: First law of thermodynamics
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Energy conservation for thermal systems: the change in internal energy equals heat added minus work done by the gas.

concepts.title: Gauss's law and flux
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The relationship between electric flux through a closed surface and the enclosed charge, used to find fields of symmetric charge distributions.

concepts.title: Heat engines and the second law
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Cyclic processes that convert heat to work, their efficiency limits, and the second law's statement about entropy and irreversibility.

concepts.title: Kinetic theory and ideal gas law
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The model of a gas as many colliding particles, connecting microscopic motion to macroscopic pressure, temperature, and the ideal gas law.

concepts.title: Lenses and mirrors (ray diagrams)
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: How converging and diverging lenses and curved mirrors form images, found with ray diagrams and the thin-lens equation.

concepts.title: Magnetic fields from currents
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Magnetic forces, magnetic fields, and Faraday's law
concepts.description: How currents in wires, loops, and solenoids generate magnetic fields, described by the Biot-Savart and Ampere relationships.

concepts.title: Magnetic force on moving charges
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Magnetic forces, magnetic fields, and Faraday's law
concepts.description: The force on a moving charge in a magnetic field, perpendicular to both velocity and field, governed by the right-hand rule.

concepts.title: Nuclear decay and mass-energy
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Radioactive decay, nuclear reactions, and the equivalence of mass and energy expressed by E = mc^2.

concepts.title: Photoelectric effect and photons
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Light ejecting electrons from a metal, explained by quantized photons with energy proportional to frequency.

concepts.title: RC circuits
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Circuits combining resistors and capacitors, where charge and current change exponentially over a characteristic time constant.

concepts.title: Refraction and Snell's law
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The bending of light as it passes between media of different refractive indices, including total internal reflection.

concepts.title: Centripetal force and acceleration
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The inward net force and acceleration required to keep an object moving in a circular path, directed toward the center.

concepts.title: Elastic and inelastic collisions
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Collisions classified by whether kinetic energy is conserved (elastic) or lost (inelastic), with momentum conserved in both.

concepts.title: Conservation of mechanical energy
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: In the absence of non-conservative forces, the total of kinetic and potential energy of a system remains constant.

concepts.title: Conservation of momentum
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The total momentum of an isolated system is conserved, allowing analysis of collisions and explosions.

concepts.title: Doppler effect
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The change in observed frequency of a wave due to relative motion between source and observer.

concepts.title: Electric charge and Coulomb's law
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Charged objects exert forces on each other described by Coulomb's law, with like charges repelling and opposites attracting.

concepts.title: Electric fields
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The region around a charge where it exerts force on other charges, visualized with field lines pointing away from positive and toward negative charges.

concepts.title: Electromagnetism (current and magnetism)
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Electric currents create magnetic fields and changing magnetic fields induce currents, linking electricity and magnetism.

concepts.title: Free-body diagrams
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Forces and Newton's laws of motion
concepts.description: Diagrams isolating a single object and showing all external forces acting on it as vectors.

concepts.title: Free fall and acceleration due to gravity
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Motion of objects under constant gravitational acceleration near Earth's surface, neglecting air resistance.

concepts.title: Friction (static and kinetic)
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Forces and Newton's laws of motion
concepts.description: Contact force opposing relative motion or tendency of motion between surfaces, characterized by coefficients of friction and the normal force.

concepts.title: Hooke's law and springs
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The restoring force of an ideal spring is proportional to its displacement from equilibrium.

concepts.title: Forces on an inclined plane
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Forces and Newton's laws of motion
concepts.description: Resolving gravity into components parallel and perpendicular to a ramp to analyze sliding and equilibrium.

concepts.title: Kinematic equations
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The equations of constant-acceleration motion relating displacement, velocity, acceleration, and time.

concepts.title: Motion graphs (x-t, v-t, a-t)
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Reading and connecting position-time, velocity-time, and acceleration-time graphs, including slope and area interpretations.

concepts.title: Kinetic and potential energy
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Energy of motion (kinetic) and energy stored due to position or configuration (gravitational and elastic potential).

concepts.title: Magnetic fields and magnets
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The region of magnetic influence around magnets and currents, visualized with field lines running from north to south pole.

concepts.title: Momentum and impulse
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Momentum is mass times velocity; impulse is the force applied over a time interval and equals the change in momentum.

concepts.title: Newton's first law (inertia)
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Forces and Newton's laws of motion
concepts.description: An object at rest stays at rest and an object in motion stays in motion at constant velocity unless acted on by a net external force.

concepts.title: Newton's law of universal gravitation
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Every mass attracts every other mass with a force proportional to the product of masses and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them.

concepts.title: Newton's second law (F = ma)
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Forces and Newton's laws of motion
concepts.description: The net force on an object equals its mass times its acceleration, defining how forces change motion.

concepts.title: Newton's third law (action-reaction)
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Forces and Newton's laws of motion
concepts.description: For every action force there is an equal and opposite reaction force acting on a different object.

concepts.title: Ohm's law and resistance
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The relationship among voltage, current, and resistance in a circuit, where voltage equals current times resistance.

concepts.title: Position, velocity, and acceleration
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The relationships among position, velocity, and acceleration for an object moving in a straight line, and how each is the rate of change of the previous.

concepts.title: Power
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The rate at which work is done or energy is transferred, measured in watts.

concepts.title: Projectile motion
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Two-dimensional motion under gravity, where horizontal and vertical motions are independent, producing a parabolic trajectory.

concepts.title: Relative velocity
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Velocity measured with respect to different reference frames, combined using vector addition (e.g. boat crossing a river).

concepts.title: Series and parallel circuits
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: How resistors combine and how current and voltage distribute differently when components are wired in series versus in parallel.

concepts.title: Simple harmonic motion
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Periodic oscillation produced by a restoring force proportional to displacement, as in a mass on a spring or a pendulum.

concepts.title: Sound as a longitudinal wave
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Sound propagates as a longitudinal pressure wave through a medium, with pitch set by frequency and loudness by amplitude.

concepts.title: Standing waves and resonance
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Stationary wave patterns with fixed nodes and antinodes formed by interfering waves on strings or in tubes at resonant frequencies.

concepts.title: Tension and pulley systems
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Forces and Newton's laws of motion
concepts.description: Analyzing connected objects linked by ropes over pulleys, where tension transmits force through the system.

concepts.title: Uniform circular motion
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Motion in a circle at constant speed, where velocity continuously changes direction and points tangent to the path.

concepts.title: Vectors and vector components
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Representing quantities with magnitude and direction, and resolving vectors into perpendicular components.

concepts.title: Wave interference and superposition
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: When waves overlap their displacements add, producing constructive and destructive interference.

concepts.title: Wave properties (wavelength, frequency, speed)
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The defining quantities of a wave and the relationship that wave speed equals frequency times wavelength.

concepts.title: Work done by a force
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Energy transferred when a force acts on an object over a displacement, equal to force times displacement times the cosine of the angle between them.

concepts.title: Two-dimensional collisions
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Applying conservation of momentum component-wise to glancing collisions in a plane.

concepts.title: Banked curves and circular motion forces
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: How banking a curve lets the normal force supply centripetal force, and the role of friction at various speeds.

concepts.title: Beats
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The slow periodic variation in amplitude that arises when two waves of slightly different frequencies superpose.

concepts.title: Calculus of motion (derivatives and integrals)
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Treating velocity as the derivative of position and acceleration as the derivative of velocity, with integrals recovering motion from acceleration.

concepts.title: Damped and driven oscillations
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: How friction damps oscillations over time and how a periodic driving force produces resonance near the natural frequency.

concepts.title: Air resistance and terminal velocity
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Forces and Newton's laws of motion
concepts.description: Velocity-dependent drag forces that grow until they balance gravity, producing a constant terminal velocity.

concepts.title: Electric dipoles and field superposition
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The field and potential of two opposite charges, and how fields from multiple charges superpose in space.

concepts.title: Electromagnetic waves and the spectrum
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Self-propagating oscillations of perpendicular electric and magnetic fields traveling at the speed of light, spanning the EM spectrum.

concepts.title: Gravitational potential energy and escape velocity
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The general form of gravitational potential energy for an inverse-square field and the speed needed to escape a body's gravity.

concepts.title: Gyroscopes and precession
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The vector nature of angular momentum and torque, producing the surprising precession of a spinning gyroscope.

concepts.title: Heat transfer (conduction, convection, radiation)
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The three mechanisms by which thermal energy moves: conduction through materials, convection by fluid motion, and radiation by electromagnetic waves.

concepts.title: Kepler's laws of planetary motion
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The three empirical laws describing elliptical orbits, equal-area sweeps, and the period-radius relationship, derived from gravitation.

concepts.title: Kirchhoff's circuit laws
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The junction (current) and loop (voltage) rules used to analyze multi-loop circuits that cannot be reduced to simple series and parallel.

concepts.title: Motional EMF and inductance
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.unit: Magnetic forces, magnetic fields, and Faraday's law
concepts.description: EMF generated by a conductor moving through a magnetic field, and the self-induced EMF of an inductor opposing changing current.

concepts.title: Nuclear binding energy, fission, and fusion
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: How the binding-energy-per-nucleon curve explains why fission of heavy nuclei and fusion of light nuclei both release energy.

concepts.title: Optical instruments and lens combinations
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: How combinations of lenses build microscopes, telescopes, and the eye, with magnification from multiple optical elements.

concepts.title: Potential energy curves and equilibrium
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Reading a potential energy versus position graph to identify stable and unstable equilibria, turning points, and force as the negative slope.

concepts.title: Wavefunctions and quantum probability
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The wavefunction encodes a particle's state, with its squared magnitude giving the probability distribution for measurements.

concepts.title: Radioactive decay and half-life
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The exponential law governing how a population of unstable nuclei decays over time, characterized by the half-life.

concepts.title: Relativistic energy and momentum
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: How energy and momentum are redefined at high speeds, including rest energy E = mc^2 and the energy-momentum relation.

concepts.title: PV diagrams and thermodynamic processes
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Visualizing isothermal, adiabatic, isobaric, and isochoric processes on a pressure-volume diagram, where area represents work.

concepts.title: Thin-film interference
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Color patterns from light reflecting off the two surfaces of a thin film, where path difference and phase shifts cause interference.

concepts.title: Time dilation and length contraction
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Consequences of the constant speed of light: moving clocks run slow and moving objects contract along the direction of motion.

concepts.title: The traveling wave equation
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: The mathematical description of a wave as a function of space and time, characterized by wave number, angular frequency, and phase.

concepts.title: Heisenberg uncertainty principle
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: A fundamental limit on the simultaneous precision of complementary quantities such as position and momentum.

concepts.title: 3D vectors, dot and cross products
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Operating on vectors in three dimensions, including dot products (projection) and cross products (perpendicular vector and area).

concepts.title: Viscosity and laminar vs turbulent flow
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Internal friction in fluids and the transition between smooth laminar flow and chaotic turbulent flow characterized by the Reynolds number.

concepts.title: Wave-particle duality and de Broglie waves
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Matter and light both exhibit wave and particle behavior; particles have a de Broglie wavelength inversely proportional to momentum.

concepts.title: Work-energy theorem and variable forces
concepts.subject: physics
concepts.description: Work as the integral of a variable force over displacement, equal to the change in kinetic energy.
